MOVING CROSS-DEFENDANTS OWED A FIDUCIARY DUTY TO "INVESTIGATE THE MATERIAL FACTS OF THE TRANSACTION, AND [NOT] TRANSMIT [INFORMATION] TO [CROSS-COMPLAINANT], WITHOUT EITHER VERIFIYING THE INFORMATION OR DISCLOSING TO THE PRINCIPAL THAT THE INFORMATION HAS NOT BEEN VERIFIED."SALAHUTDIN V. VALLEY OF CALIFORNIA, INC. (1994) 24 CAL. APP.4TH 555, 562-63. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING WHETHER CROSS-DEFENDANTS BREACHED THIS DUTY. SEE HOWARD MULLINS AND SUSAN MULLINS' SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED AND DISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS IN OPPOSITION TO CROSS-DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION ('MULLINGS' SEPARATE STATEMENT IN OPPOSITION"), SPECIFICALLY, OPPOSING PARTIES' RESPONSE TO UMF 7 AND EVIDENCE CITED THEREIN. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING WHETHER CROSS-COMPLAINANTS SUFFERED ANY DAMAGES AS A RESULT OF THE BREACH. SEE SUSAN MULLINS DECL. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING WHETHER CROSS-DEFENDANTS' ACTIONS WARRANT PUNITIVE DAMAGES. SEE MULLINS' SEPARATE STATEMENT IN OPPOSITION, RESPONSE TO UMF 7 AND EVIDENCE CITED HEREIN. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS HOWARD MULLIN AND SUSAN MULLIN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THE MULLINS SHIFTED THE BURDEN, BUT CROSS-DEFENDANTS MALKASSIANS HAVE RAISED TRIABLE ISSUES REGARDING WHETHER A MISREPRESENTATION WAS ACTUALLY MADE, WHETHER THERE WAS INTENT TO DEFRAUD, AND WHETHER THERE WAS JUSTIFIABLE RELIANCE. CROSS-DEFENDANTS HAVE PRESENTED EVIDENCE THAT THE MULLINS KNEW, EITHER DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY THROUGH THE MULLINS' AGENT, THAT THE MALKASSIANS WERE NOT REPRESENTED BY A BROKER. IF THE MULLINS KNEW THIS FACT, THEN IT COULD NOT HAVE BEEN MISREPRESENTED, AND THE MULLINS COULD NOT HAVE JUSTIFIABLY RELIED ON ANY MISREPRESENTATION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS HOWARD MULLIN, SUSAN MULLIN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THE MULLINS SHIFTED THE BURDEN, BUT CROSS-DEFENDANTS MALKASSIANS HAVE RAISED TRIABLE ISSUES REGARDING WHETHER A MISREPRESENTATION WAS ACTUALLY MADE, WHETHER THERE WAS INTENT TO DEFRAUD, AND WHETHER THERE WAS JUSTIFIABLE RELIANCE. CROSS-DEFENDANTS HAVE PRESENTED EVIDENCE THAT THE MULLINS KNEW, EITHER DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY THROUGH THE MULLINS' AGENT, THAT THE MALKASSIANS WERE NOT REPRESENTED BY A BROKER. MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JEFFREY BELOTE, ESQ., 415.984.8500, FOR PLAINTIFFS/CRPSS DEFENDANTS MALKASSIAN.
SHANNON JONES, ESQ., AND JESSICA TAKANO, ESQ., FOR DEFENDANTS CHARLENE ATTARD AND BRPS LLC.
CARLETON BRIGGS, ESQ., 707.523.2251, FOR DEFENDANTS/CROSSCOMPLAINANTS HOWARD MULLIN AND SUSAN MULLIN.

RULING: AT BEGINNING OF HEARING, THE COURT ISSUES WRITTEN TENTATIVE RULING. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT AS TO CROSS-DEFENDANTS WILLIAM FACENDINI AND FERRYLE MCOMBER (ISSUES FOUR, FIVE, SEVEN AND EIGHT). THESE CROSS DEFENDANTS WERE DISMISSED ON APRIL 25, 2013. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND THE ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO ALL REMAINING ISSUES. MOVING CROSS-DEFENDANTS OWED A FIDUCIARY DUTY TO "INVESTIGATE THE MATERIAL FACTS OF THE TRANSACTION, AND [NOT] TRANSMIT [INFORMATION] TO [CROSS-COMPLAINANT], WITHOUT EITHER VERIFIYING THE INFORMATION OR DISCLOSING TO THE PRINCIPAL THAT THE INFORMATION HAS NOT BEEN VERIFIED."SALAHUTDIN V. VALLEY OF CALIFORNIA, INC. (1994) 24 CAL. APP.4TH 555, 562-63. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING WHETHER CROSS-DEFENDANTS BREACHED THIS DUTY. SEE HOWARD MULLINS AND SUSAN MULLINS' SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED AND DISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS IN OPPOSITION TO CROSS-DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION ('MULLINGS' SEPARATE STATEMENT IN OPPOSITION"), SPECIFICALLY, OPPOSING PARTIES' RESPONSE TO UMF 7 AND EVIDENCE CITED THEREIN. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING WHETHER CROSS-COMPLAINANTS SUFFERED ANY DAMAGES AS A RESULT OF THE BREACH. SEE SUSAN MULLINS DECL. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING WHETHER CROSS-DEFENDANTS' ACTIONS WARRANT PUNITIVE DAMAGES. SEE MULLINS' SEPARATE STATEMENT IN OPPOSITION, RESPONSE TO UMF 7 AND EVIDENCE CITED HEREIN. CROSS-DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR FEES AND COSTS IS GRANTED IN PART. IN ACCORD WITH JUDGE ROBERTSON'S ORDER DATED DECEMBER 6, 2012, CROSS-COMPLAINANTS ARE ORDERED TO PAY DEFENDANTS FOR THE FILING FEES FOR THIS MOTION.
(Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: marla miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
